name: Locked out user sees error message on login attempt
preconditions: The Sauce Demo application is accessible and running
The account 'locked_out_user' exists in the system and is in a locked-out state
The browser is open and no active session exists (user is logged out)

test_data.expected_error_message: Epic sadface: Sorry, this user has been locked out.

steps.action: Navigate to the Sauce Demo login page at the base URL
steps.expected: The Login Page is displayed with a Username field, Password field, and Login button visible

steps.action: Enter the locked-out username into the Username field
steps.expected: The text 'locked_out_user' appears in the Username input field

steps.action: Enter the password into the Password field
steps.expected: The password is masked and entered into the Password input field
The form is submitted; no redirect to the inventory or any other application page occurs

steps.action: Verify the current URL remains on the login page
steps.expected: The URL does not change from the base login URL (no navigation to '/inventory.html' or any other page)

steps.action: Verify an error message container is visible on the page
steps.expected: A red error banner or message container is displayed on the Login Page

steps.action: Verify the exact text content of the error message
steps.test_data: Epic sadface: Sorry, this user has been locked out.
steps.expected: The error message reads exactly: 'Epic sadface: Sorry, this user has been locked out.'

steps.action: Verify the Username and Password fields display error highlight styling
steps.expected: Both the Username and Password input fields are outlined or highlighted in red to indicate an error state

steps.action: Verify error icon indicators are present on the Username and Password fields
steps.expected: An error icon (e.g., an 'X' or warning symbol) is visible inside or adjacent to both input fields

steps.action: Click the X (close) button on the error message banner
steps.expected: The error message banner is dismissed and no longer visible on the page

steps.action: Verify the page remains on the Login Page after dismissing the error
steps.expected: The Login Page is still displayed with Username and Password fields and the Login button; the user has not been redirected

postconditions: The user remains unauthenticated and on the Login Page
No session or authentication token has been created for locked_out_user
The application state is unchanged from before the test
